invariant_definition: Liberation attained at the falling-away of the body — the standard *post-mortem* liberation of all schools. In Advaita, *videha-mukti* is the second moment of liberation following *jīvanmukti* (the *prārabdha*-residue exhausts itself with the body). In Viśiṣṭādvaita, Dvaita, and Vaiṣṇava bhedābheda, *videha-mukti* is the only liberation; the soul ascends to Vaikuṇṭha or its analogue.

per_school.definition: The exhaustion of *prārabdha-karma* with the body's death; for the *jīvanmukta* this is merely the cessation of phenomenal continuance. For the non-*jīvanmukta jñāni* it is the moment of liberation proper.
The unique liberation: the soul leaves the body, traverses the *arcirādi-mārga* (the path of light), and reaches Vaikuṇṭha (V) or the appropriate *taratamya*-graded abode (D).
